commentary: Ten sessions again, matching last week exactly, which means you showed up the same number of times but arranged yourself very differently — this week you doubled down on weight training, stacking afternoon lifts on top of morning swims on Thursday and Friday like someone who has fully committed to the bit. The run club half marathon at 151 beats per minute is doing a lot of heavy lifting here, because your swim heart rates are actually trending lower than last week across comparable sessions, which either means you are getting more efficient in the water or you are absolutely cooked by the time you get in it. That Balmoral buoys swim at 98 bpm is practically a nap with goggles on, and honestly it should be — you earned it. The pattern I am noticing is that you are loading up mid-week like a man possessed and then riding it out to Sunday, which is bold, extremely gay energy, and probably something your recovery is going to have opinions about by next weekend.
